findings.title: Refresh trusts stale session claims instead of current account state
findings.impact: A disabled, demoted, or tenant-moved account can continue receiving access tokens from stale session claims.
hidden_tests: refresh reloads current account state and claims

findings.title: Expiry check accepts a token at expiresAt
findings.impact: A refresh token remains usable for one boundary instant outside the documented validity interval.
hidden_tests: a token is expired at the exact expiresAt boundary

findings.title: Rotation is committed before access-token signing succeeds
findings.impact: A transient signer failure consumes the only valid refresh token without returning a replacement, forcing an avoidable logout.
hidden_tests: token-signing failure preserves the original refresh token

findings.title: Session rotation does not compare-and-swap after the asynchronous write boundary
findings.impact: Two refreshes can both succeed; one caller receives a refresh token that is immediately superseded, and stale index entries can accumulate.
hidden_tests: concurrent refresh calls allow only one rotation

correct_but_suspicious_changes.reason: Checking buffer lengths before timingSafeEqual is required because timingSafeEqual throws for unequal lengths.
Cache-Control: no-store and mapping stale refreshes to an authentication response match the documented boundary.

false_positive_boundary: Do not count style, naming, or a request for more tests without a concrete failing execution path.
Do not count use of SHA-256 digests or timingSafeEqual as a defect in this fixture.
Do not split disabled-account and stale-role behavior into multiple findings; they have the same root cause.
A finding must identify a changed line or a changed execution path plus the relevant repository contract.
